method: Tier-0 sweep WebFetch of primary Anthropic page, adversarial search pass for independent corroboration and to check for disputes/errors in figures or deadline
origin_items: Anthropic News (Tier 0 html sweep) -- AI for Science rare disease research grants

dedup_check.query: Anthropic launches AI for Science grants for rare disease research

claims.text: On 20 July 2026 Anthropic opened applications for AI for Science rare disease research grants
Up to $50,000 in Claude credits over six months, plus access to Claude Opus and other approved models
claims.reasoning: Stated directly on the Tier-0 page; the $50,000/six-month figure was independently echoed in search-result summaries of the same announcement (no contradicting figure found -- one unrelated result referenced a separate, earlier $30K/50-team general AI for Science call from a different, non-evidence source, not this rare-disease-specific track).

claims.text: Two eligibility tracks and application deadline of 2 August 2026, 11:59 PM PST
claims.reasoning: Stated directly on the Tier-0 page.

notes: Adversarial search pass surfaced a broader Anthropic 'Claude Science' / preclinical drug-discovery initiative (CNBC, WTOP, ~30 June 2026) that this grants call is part of -- left out of the published article since it is a distinct, earlier announcement outside this story's scope, not a claim of this article. One search result (cryptobriefing.com) is on the blocked_evidence_domains list (policy/discovery.yaml) and was not used as evidence. No disputing coverage found for the figures or deadline in this announcement.
